evidence_quotes.quote: Personalised Targetting of the addicted with for example showing gambling addicts more gambling ads.
to stop bussinesses from killing games by pulling the plug on (mostly older) games

paragraphs.text: To start of, I like the idea of consumer protection online like stopping practice 3 (Personalised Targetting of the addicted with for example showing gambling addicts more gambling ads.) I support the majority of the protection that this act is promising for the people! Next to having my support I would like to ask for the adding of another regulation, to stop bussinesses from killing games by pulling the plug on (mostly older) games / stopping to support it due to having "No end of life plan.". I myself have bought Need for Speed Rivals and the game it's support is ending soon. A decent portion of the game I and many others paid for will become innaccesible! This is a problem as I paid for this product and now a large part of it is being taken away from many. And no alternative is being offered like players hosting a server themself or being able to buy hosting services to run (public) servers for the game to still be able to acces content that was paid for. I am asking for a policy to be added into the act that forces the game industry to always make it an option at the end of a game it's life to let the community be able to host their own servers, so they can keep playing the game they paid for instead of having it (partially or fully) shutdown.
